title: Conversion therapies
subtitle: Therapies attempting to change a person’s sexual orientation or gender identity included. They can entail counseling, drugging, electric shocks, castrations, or brain surgery.
caption: Across the globe in 2025, the legal status of conversion therapies—practices attempting to change a person's sexual orientation or gender identity—varies significantly by country. The map categorizes nations into those where such therapies are banned, those where the legality varies by region, and those where they are not banned. A large portion of the world, including most of Africa, the Middle East, Russia, and much of Asia, falls into the "not banned" category, shown in red. In contrast, bans are concentrated in specific regions: most of South America, Canada, several Western European nations like France and Germany, and Australia and New Zealand are colored blue to indicate a prohibition. A smaller group of countries, including the United States, India, and Greenland, are shaded light green, signifying that the legality of these practices varies by region within their borders. The most prominent pattern is the widespread absence of a national ban, with prohibitions clustered primarily in the Americas and Western Europe.
